summary: Excellent FM‑broadcast recording with crisp sound and a legendary Estimated > Shakedown, Jack Straw and Sugaree despite Garcia’s raspy voice
review: The show captures one of the Dead’s high‑energy late‑1978 performances – a blistering first set highlighted by Jack Straw and Sugaree and a second set dominated by an unforgettable Estimated > Shakedown leading into Fire on the Mountain.  The FM broadcast source is clean enough for modern ears, though a brief cut in Shakedown and occasional vocal strain are noted; overall the music more than makes up for any technical hiccups.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (30+) are 4‑5 stars, praising both the performance and the FM broadcast sound; only a few note minor flaws, giving the recording an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: FM broadcast mix is clear and well balanced – reviewers call it “great sound” and “sound is everything I desired.”  A few mention a brief cut in the Shakedown intro and occasional drop‑outs, but no major distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set launches with a punchy Jack Straw and Sugaree; the second set builds momentum with an iconic Estimated > Shakedown, followed by a soaring Fire on the Mountain and other classics.  Energy stays high throughout despite Garcia’s hoarse vocals.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s voice is described as raspy, congested or “shot,” but his guitar work and leads (especially on Passenger and Sugaree) are praised as stellar.
band_member_comments.Drums: Guest percussionist Hamza al‑Din receives strong praise for his tight drumming during the Drums, Ollin Arranged, and Fire segments.
